purpose: Track whether each benchmark id can produce real score JSON through the TaskOps runner. Stubs are smoke-only and do not count as benchmark results.

status_vocab.runnable_after_setup: Public harness/scorer exists; adapter work is needed but the benchmark can plausibly produce real scores.
status_vocab.blocked_until_pinned: The benchmark is useful, but a public harness, task split, evaluator, or exact reproduction contract is not pinned enough for a real score run.
status_vocab.blocked_operationally: A public harness exists, but local/cloud services, accounts, VMs, or compute requirements must be resolved before running.
status_vocab.reported_only: Use only as contextual reported score until a reproducible harness is available.

benchmarks.scoring_path: Generate model patches/predictions, then run SWE-bench evaluation in Docker or submit through sb-cli/cloud evaluation. Primary score is resolved_percent/pass rate.
adapter_work: Create dataset subset selector for Verified
Invoke direct_agent or taskops_agent to produce patch predictions
Convert predictions to SWE-bench expected format
Run official evaluator or sb-cli
Parse resolved percentage into score.primary
benchmarks.notes: Best first coding control adapter because official evaluation path is public and well-known.

benchmarks.scoring_path: Run the public SWE-bench Pro harness/evaluator on selected tasks and parse resolved_percent.
adapter_work: Pin the public task split used for the paper run
Generate patch predictions through the selected agent arm
Run Docker-based evaluator
Parse resolved percentage into score.primary
benchmarks.notes: Qwen reported a refined public set, so reproduction must explicitly pin the public subset used.

benchmarks.scoring_path: Install Harbor, verify oracle solutions for Terminal-Bench 2.0, run agent on selected tasks, and parse Harbor/Terminal-Bench success results.
adapter_work: Install Harbor and Terminal-Bench 2.x task set
Implement a Harbor-compatible agent wrapper for direct_agent/taskops_agent
Run selected tasks in Docker-backed terminal environments
Parse task success rate into score.primary
benchmarks.notes: Strong TaskOps fit because tasks are terminal-native and evidence/log heavy.

benchmarks.scoring_path: Install BenchFlow/SkillsBench, run selected tasks through the BenchFlow runtime, and parse per-task success results.
adapter_work: Install benchflow 0.6.x with uv
Clone and sync SkillsBench
Implement compatible agent invocation for the benchmark
Run selected self-contained tasks
Parse average success rate into score.primary
benchmarks.notes: Good procedural/skill-following benchmark, but adapter must respect SkillsBench's expected agent interface.

benchmarks.scoring_path: Run the NL2RepoBench task environment and pytest/evaluation checks against generated repositories; normalize benchmark-specific score.
adapter_work: Pin the exact NL2RepoBench repo/task split
Generate repositories through direct_agent/taskops_agent
Run task-specific tests/evaluator
Parse pass/fail or continuous reward into score.primary
benchmarks.notes: Public repo exists, but adapter must inspect actual evaluator commands before this can be marked configured.

benchmarks.scoring_path: Use ALE task cards and reference evaluators for the curated free/easy subset, excluding licensed/GPU-heavy tasks, then normalize task-specific scores.
adapter_work: Clone ALE and map data/ale_free_easy_subset.json task ids to task cards
Verify which selected tasks can run on local Linux/free tools without extra licensed software
Create per-task workspace setup and artifact collection
Run ALE evaluators or reference checks
Normalize task-specific hidden-reference scores into score.primary
benchmarks.notes: High-value TaskOps benchmark, but operational setup must be pinned before claiming runnable_now.

benchmarks.scoring_path: Use the public HCAST task source code subset and task-specific grading scripts if available; full-scale suite requires METR access.
adapter_work: Inspect public task folders for runnable graders
Select public tasks with local reproducibility
Wrap the task standard in TaskOps/agent invocation
Parse task success vs human-calibrated horizon metadata
benchmarks.notes: The public repo is useful, but large-scale/full-suite evaluation is not directly public.

benchmarks.scoring_path: Run RE-Bench task environments and benchmark-specific scoring against agent-generated research engineering artifacts.
adapter_work: Clone RE-Bench and inspect task runtime requirements
Pin a small selected subset due to compute/time cost
Run agent attempts with explicit wall-clock budgets
Parse benchmark-specific score vs expert baseline
benchmarks.notes: Excellent long-work benchmark but not a cheap first adapter.

benchmarks.scoring_path: Full ALE evaluation across broad task types and reference graders.
adapter_work: Resolve licensed/professional/GPU/Windows task requirements
Pin task subset and environments
Run task-specific graders
benchmarks.notes: Keep full ALE as extension; use ALE-FreeEasy for the practical first slice.

benchmarks.scoring_path: Run TheAgentCompany's self-hosted workplace environment and checkpoint/execution-based evaluation.
adapter_work: Bring up required services such as GitLab/OwnCloud/RocketChat equivalents
Implement browser/program/communication-capable agent adapter
Run selected tasks and parse checkpoint scores
benchmarks.notes: Good extension benchmark; too operationally heavy for the first score-producing adapter.

benchmarks.scoring_path: Long-horizon vending business simulation scored by business outcome, if public scenario/evaluator code is pinned.
adapter_work: Find or obtain public evaluator/scenario code
Pin environment and scoring definition
Wrap long-horizon simulation in TaskOps
benchmarks.notes: Currently better as motivation/extension than core reproducible score data.

benchmarks.scoring_path: No public harness/task set pinned in this repo yet.
adapter_work: Find public benchmark definition or replace with a reproducible multilingual SWE benchmark
benchmarks.notes: Keep reported-only until public reproduction path is available.
